name: Fish Steamed in Napa Cabbage - p Cooking Cla
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------FOR THE SAUCE-----------------------
1. Combine 1/2 cup seafood stock, roasted red bell peppers, lemon juice, anchovy paste or salt, white pepper in a blender or food processor with a steel blade.
2. Puree until smooth and pour into a small saucepan.
3. Bring to a boil over medium heat and reduce by half, stirring frequently.
--------------------------FOR THE FISH:----------------------------
1. Blanch the cabbage leaves in boiling water for about 2 minutes, then drain and set aside.
2. Cut salmon and sole into 1/2-inch cubes and set aside.
3. Heat oil in a small skillet over medium heat. Add shallots and garlic, sauteing frequently for 5 minutes.
4. Mix the sauteed shallot-garlic mixture with fish, lemon juice, pepper, salt, thyme, and basil.
5. Place two overlapping cabbage leaves on each of six bamboo steamer plates.
6. Divide the filling mixture into the center of the cabbage leaf sets and roll up, tucking in the sides to enclose the filling. Secure rolls with toothpicks.
7. Steam over boiling water for 7 to 10 minutes.
8. Serve with a pool of sauce, topping each plate with a cabbage roll.
9. Note: Cabbage rolls can be refrigerated for one day before cooking, tightly covered with plastic wrap. Do not steam until just prior to serving. The sauce can also be prepared and refrigerated in advance, reheating over low flame and stirring occasionally.
